rationale: The query's engine is an exact mean-field/self-consistent solution for a phase-transition threshold on a heterogeneous-degree network, where the transition is anomalous (vanishing threshold, higher-order transition, strong size effects) specifically when a degree-distribution moment (second/fourth moment) diverges — hyp_abstract preserves this exact dependency on tie-distribution moments. The candidate instead analyzes a committed-minority/naming-game consensus model whose critical parameter is the fraction of zealot agents p_c (~10%), with the transition manifesting as a scaling change in consensus time T_c(N); network topology (ER, scale-free) is only checked for qualitative robustness, not used to derive p_c from degree-distribution moments, so the core machinery does not match. The object also differs from the query: the candidate concerns opinion/consensus dynamics among social agents (matching the social-science target field) rather than Ising spins on a random graph, so object_difference is true.
